content: 2024–2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems can be reframed as layered, curiosity-pruned collective agents themselves, where each memory chunk acts as an autonomous micro-agent that actively votes on which new experiences the parent agent should prioritize labeling and retaining for future learning. This creates a decentralized active learning loop that outperforms centralized memory querying by aligning memory retention with inherent curiosity signals.
